questions.question: Write an algorithm to find all connected components of an undirected graph using DFS. Illustrate with an example.
keyPoints: Outer loop iterates through all vertices
For each unvisited vertex, start DFS -- all visited vertices form one component
Use visited array shared across all DFS calls
Component counter incremented each time a new DFS starts
Example with a disconnected graph showing 2-3 components
Time complexity: O(V + E)

questions.question: Define connected component. For a given graph, identify all connected components and the number of edges in each.
keyPoints: Definition: maximal connected subgraph
Identify vertex sets for each component
Count edges within each component
Verify: minimum edges per component = vertices - 1

questions.question: Explain the difference between connected components in undirected graphs and strongly connected components in directed graphs.
keyPoints: Connected component: path between every pair (undirected)
Strongly connected component: directed path between every pair (directed)
SCC algorithms: Kosaraju's, Tarjan's
A directed graph can have multiple SCCs even if weakly connected
Example comparing both concepts

questions.question: Write a C program to count the number of connected components in an undirected graph using BFS.
keyPoints: Graph representation using adjacency list
BFS function with queue and visited array
Main loop calling BFS for each unvisited vertex
Counter tracks number of BFS calls = number of components
Output the component count and vertices in each component
